---
title: "timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_aa"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-28
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [7, 23, 16, 16, 20, 18]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![2x3 grid of day-5-vs-day-0 amino-acid Fisher volcano panels: rows are conditions (top = BWM, bottom = control); columns are ribosome sites (A / P / E). Each panel plots -log10(FDR) against log2(odds ratio); triangles mark FDR<0.05 hits and circles are non-significant. Glyphs are coloured by amino-acid property (acidic = red, basic = blue, hydrophobic = green, neutral = orange, polar = purple).](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/composite/aa_fisher_composite.png)

Each glyph is a single amino acid at one (condition, site) BH-FDR family of 20; horizontal dashed line is FDR = 0.05 and vertical dashed lines mark +/-0.5 log2(OR). Positive log2(OR) means day-5-enriched relative to day-0 *within the same condition*; negative means day-5-depleted relative to day-0. The test does not compare BWM to control directly — that contrast is implied by reading paired top/bottom rows of the grid side-by-side. Panels are arranged in A / P / E ribosome-site order across columns.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_aa.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_aa.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_codon.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_aa.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_codon.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`
- **Test:** Fisher's exact (two-sided), day-5-vs-day-0 2x2 contingency per (condition, site, aa); BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of 20 AAs
- **Design:** Per (condition, site, aa) 2x2 day-5-vs-day-0 contingency tested by two-sided Fisher's exact within one condition; replicates summed before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR applied independently within each of 6 (condition, site) families of 20 AAs (120 tests total). The test is **within-condition**: BWM and control are tested separately and never share a contingency.

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 biological reps per (condition, timepoint) are summed into the 2x2 before Fisher's exact; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative** — pooled (day_5 + day_0) totals at the condition level are in the thousands; this CSV carries the family's most extreme case, control,E,P at the family-wide minimum `p_adj` on a `log2_OR` of -0.63 (~35% relative depletion). Read `log2_OR` as the primary effect column, not p magnitude.
- **bh-per-(condition,site)** — BH-FDR is computed independently within each of the 6 (condition, site) families of 20 AAs; `p_adj` means "corrected within this sub-family", not across the full 120-row file.
- **within-condition-clean** — unlike the between-condition or between-timepoint Wilcoxon families, no condition pooling and no timepoint pooling cross-contaminate the test; the design is structurally cleaner.

---

## Headline

d5 vs d0 within-condition Fisher (aa-level): 36/120 hits at FDR<0.05 (15/60 BWM, 21/60 control). Largest-magnitude divergence cell: E:K — BWM E:K `log2_OR`=-0.42 vs control E:K `log2_OR`=+0.26. Cross-contrast hook: the d10_vs_d0_aa cell (BWM `log2_OR`=-0.34 / control `log2_OR`=+0.18) carries similar direction and magnitude; the d10_vs_d5_aa sister has E:K null in both conditions (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.08 ns / control `log2_OR`=-0.08 ns) — downstream reading should weigh whether the numerical sequence supports a single d0-anchored pattern vs other readings. Largest-magnitude shared-direction cells: A:N enriched in both (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.83 vs control `log2_OR`=+0.33); A:W depleted in both (BWM `log2_OR`=-0.70 vs control `log2_OR`=-0.74 — low-count flagged); A:Y depleted in both (BWM `log2_OR`=-0.46 vs control `log2_OR`=-0.40). Other large-magnitude divergence cells: A:K (BWM `log2_OR`=-0.17 ns vs control `log2_OR`=+0.33) and E:A (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.02 ns vs control `log2_OR`=-0.36).

## Top hits

The effect column is `log2_OR` (log2 of the Fisher odds ratio for the day-5-vs-day-0 contrast within one condition; positive = day-5-enriched relative to day-0, negative = day-5-depleted). Each row pairs the BWM and control `log2_OR` for one site x amino-acid cell. The `effect change` column is the BWM `log2_OR` minus the control `log2_OR`: it measures how differently the two conditions shifted from day 0 to day 5. A value near zero means both conditions moved together (shared timecourse drift); a large-magnitude value means the two conditions' day-5-vs-day-0 trajectories diverge -- positive when BWM rose relative to control, negative when control rose relative to BWM. The three sections split every cell significant at FDR<0.05 in at least one condition by which condition(s) reach significance: both, BWM only, control only. Within each section rows are grouped by `site` in canonical A / P / E order, then sorted by `effect change` descending. Cells significant in neither condition are omitted. Each amino-acid one-letter code is expanded to `<letter> (<full name>)` on first appearance in each section.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in both conditions

| site | amino acid | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | N (asparagine) | +0.830 | +0.330 | +0.500 |  |
| A | W (tryptophan) | -0.699 | -0.735 | +0.036 | low-count (BWM, C) |
| A | Y (tyrosine) | -0.463 | -0.399 | -0.063 |  |
| P | K (lysine) | -0.333 | -0.184 | -0.149 |  |
| E | R (arginine) | -0.398 | +0.179 | -0.577 |  |
| E | K (lysine) | -0.419 | +0.256 | -0.675 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in BWM only

| site | amino acid | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | F (phenylalanine) | +0.424 | -0.014 | +0.438 |  |
| A | D (aspartate) | +0.379 | +0.049 | +0.330 |  |
| A | G (glycine) | -0.286 | -0.023 | -0.264 |  |
| P | Q (glutamine) | +0.370 | -0.086 | +0.456 |  |
| P | G (glycine) | -0.285 | -0.068 | -0.217 |  |
| P | R (arginine) | -0.285 | +0.141 | -0.427 |  |
| E | Q (glutamine) | +0.589 | +0.177 | +0.412 |  |
| E | S (serine) | +0.512 | +0.128 | +0.384 |  |
| E | G (glycine) | -0.261 | +0.044 | -0.305 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in control only

| site | amino acid | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | P (proline) | -0.058 | -0.367 | +0.309 |  |
| A | L (leucine) | -0.049 | -0.354 | +0.305 |  |
| A | A (alanine) | -0.174 | -0.288 | +0.113 |  |
| A | I (isoleucine) | +0.206 | +0.319 | -0.112 |  |
| A | E (glutamate) | -0.010 | +0.140 | -0.149 |  |
| A | K (lysine) | -0.173 | +0.327 | -0.500 |  |
| P | P (proline) | +0.183 | -0.302 | +0.486 |  |
| P | M (methionine) | -0.138 | -0.403 | +0.265 | low-count (C) |
| P | H (histidine) | -0.105 | -0.332 | +0.227 |  |
| P | E (glutamate) | +0.220 | +0.151 | +0.069 |  |
| P | V (valine) | -0.007 | +0.264 | -0.270 |  |
| E | P (proline) | -0.120 | -0.628 | +0.508 |  |
| E | T (threonine) | +0.235 | -0.232 | +0.468 |  |
| E | A (alanine) | +0.023 | -0.355 | +0.378 |  |
| E | E (glutamate) | +0.106 | +0.232 | -0.127 |  |

### Flag glossary

- `low-count` — at least one timepoint cell for the named condition arm has `day_X_count` below the test's stability threshold (typically < 100 in BWM, < 200 in control); the parenthetical suffix names which arm(s) tripped it, where `BWM` = BWM and `C` = control (so `(BWM, C)` means both arms are low, `(C)` means only control is). Applied here to A:W (both arms: BWM 108 vs 89, control 93 vs 382) and control,P,M (186 vs 607). `log2_OR` estimates and CIs are unstable at small k; magnitude weight should account for the count rather than rank by `log2_OR` alone. **Threshold asymmetry rationale:** BWM_total ~6k vs control_total ~28k (~4.55:1); the 100/200 split is heuristic, calibrated to catch rare AAs (W, C, M) in both arms — symmetric (100/100) under-flags control's rare cells (which sit at 150-300), proportional (100/450) over-flags. Fisher OR variance is dominated by the smallest cell ($\approx 1/a + 1/b + 1/c + 1/d$), so absolute count — not proportion — is the relevant axis. Codon resolution stays symmetric at 50/50 because the AA-vs-codon dilution narrows the relative cell-size spread.

## Numbers at a glance
- `n_tests`: 120 (60 per condition; BH families of 20 AAs per (condition, site))
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 36 (BWM 15, control 21)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 51 (BWM 23, control 28)
- `min adjusted-p`: 2.27e-12 (control, E, P — the file minimum and the family-wide minimum across `timepoint_fisher_within_condition`)
- `p_floor`: n/a — Fisher with pooled N in the thousands has no meaningful floor; the dominant statistical-design concern is `large-N-Fisher-anticonservative` (family-wide), not floor.
- Per (condition, site) at FDR<0.05:
  - BWM,A: 6 sig (N enriched, D enriched, F enriched; Y depleted, G depleted, W depleted); min p_adj = 3.27e-08
  - BWM,P: 4 sig (Q enriched; K depleted, G depleted, R depleted); min p_adj = 2.13e-04
  - BWM,E: 5 sig (Q enriched, S enriched; K depleted, R depleted, G depleted); min p_adj = 1.95e-09
  - control,A: 9 sig (K enriched, I enriched, N enriched, E enriched; L depleted, Y depleted, W depleted, P depleted, A depleted); min p_adj = 6.26e-08
  - control,P: 6 sig (V enriched, E enriched; P depleted, M depleted, K depleted, H depleted); min p_adj = 5.84e-04
  - control,E: 6 sig (K enriched, E enriched, R enriched; P depleted, A depleted, T depleted); min p_adj = 2.27e-12

## Biological interpretation

**The E:K direction flip is the file's headline biological cell and reproduces the d10_vs_d0_aa sister (concrete).** BWM depletes Lys at the E site between d0 and d5 (`log2_OR`=-0.42) while control enriches Lys at the same site over the same window (`log2_OR`=+0.26). Both rows move in opposite directions, and the same direction flip appears in `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_aa` (BWM `log2_OR`=-0.34 / control `log2_OR`=+0.18) — so the d0-to-d5 step carries the divergence and the d5-to-d10 step plateaus (in the d10_vs_d5 sister CSV E:K is null in both conditions). Mechanistically, Lys at the E site is a textbook anchor for ribosome-pause biology: the E-site tRNA accommodates the *previous* peptide bond's amino acid, and Lys carries a positive side-chain that can interact with the negatively charged exit tunnel — sustained or imbalanced Lys-at-E occupancy is the canonical electrostatic-stalling readout. A BWM-specific depletion combined with a control-specific enrichment between d0 and d5 means the two lines are diverging in their E-site Lys steady state across the early-timepoint transition — consistent with a perturbation-driven shift in ribosome-pausing biology at this anchor.

**A:N is the file's strongest shared-direction divergence-magnitude cell at d5, but the trajectory diverges by d10 (concrete).** BWM enriches Asn at the A site (`log2_OR`=+0.83) and so does control (`log2_OR`=+0.33) — same direction at d5, same anchor, with BWM ~2.5x larger in magnitude. The sister CSVs show the two arms part ways after d5: BWM,A,N stays elevated at d10_vs_d0 (`log2_OR`=+0.83, identical magnitude — d5-to-d10 plateau at BWM `log2_OR`~=0 ns in d10_vs_d5), while control,A,N reverses course (d10_vs_d0 `log2_OR`=-0.22 near-sig at `p_adj`=0.053, driven by a d10_vs_d5 depletion at control `log2_OR`=-0.55). So the shared-direction enrichment is a d5-only co-occurrence: BWM locks in the elevation through d10, control peaks at d5 and falls back below baseline by d10. Mechanistically, the d0-to-d5 step is consistent with a coordinated A-site Asn shift in both conditions, but only BWM sustains the shift — control's reversal points to a transient early response rather than a perturbation-amplified version of the same biology. Worth carrying into the d5_vs_d0_codon sister to ask whether AAC, AAT, or both Asn codons carry the BWM-sustained signal.

**Basic and polar features dominate the BWM,E divergence pattern; Q and S enrichment paired with K and R depletion is internally consistent (concrete).** BWM,E carries five FDR<0.05 cells in this contrast: K depleted (`log2_OR`=-0.42) and R depleted (`log2_OR`=-0.40) on the basic side, Q enriched (`log2_OR`=+0.59) and S enriched (`log2_OR`=+0.51) on the polar side, and G depleted (`log2_OR`=-0.26, the smallest-magnitude cell in the BWM,E significant set). The basic/polar redistribution at E is internally consistent in the sense that the E-site is shifting *away* from basic residues (which would relieve exit-tunnel electrostatic interactions) and *toward* uncharged polar residues — directionally consistent with reduced exit-tunnel stalling pressure in BWM over the d0-to-d5 window. Control's E site shows the opposite pattern (K enriched, plus E enriched), so the two arms are not just diverging on Lys but on the basic/polar partition more broadly. Worth carrying into the within-condition binomial baseline (does BWM,E start with a different basic/polar partition at d0 than control,E?) and into the codon sister to ask whether AAG vs AAA carries the K story.

**Control,E,P depletion is the family-wide minimum `p_adj` but is `large-N-Fisher-anticonservative`-amplified (concrete; effect size is modest).** Control depletes Pro at the E site between d0 and d5 — by far the smallest p_adj in the entire `timepoint_fisher_within_condition` family — but the underlying `log2_OR` is -0.63, which is only ~35% relative depletion. The per-CSV `control-E-P-extreme-anticonservative` caveat sits exactly here: the cell carries a tiny p magnitude because the pooled total at control over d0+d5 is in the thousands, so a 35% depletion against a very large denominator returns a microscopic Fisher p without representing a large biological effect. Pro at the E site is biologically anchored — Pro is a polyproline-context stalling driver (eIF5A/EF-P-dependent), and the *amino acid at the E site reflects the previous round's peptide-bond chemistry*, so a control-only E-Pro depletion across the d0-to-d5 transition is consistent with control's ribosome population shifting *out* of poly-proline pausing contexts. Whether this is biology (control adapting away from polyproline pausing as cells age) or artefact (mild compositional drift amplified into a tiny p by large N) is not separable from this file alone; the modest -0.63 `log2_OR` and the explicit anti-conservatism flag argue for reading direction-only and capping the rhetoric.

**The A-site Lys flip (BWM ns vs control enriched) and the W low-count shared depletion are the cleaner cross-contrast hooks (exploratory).** A:K mirrors the E:K BWM-depleted/control-enriched flip at a different ribosome site: BWM trends depleted (`log2_OR`=-0.17, near-sig at `p_adj`=0.056 per the `control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction` caveat below) while control is decisively enriched (`log2_OR`=+0.33) — a "near-flip" because BWM does not quite clear FDR<0.05. Whether the A-site and E-site Lys signals are driven by the same underlying mechanism (e.g. a tRNA-Lys isoacceptor pool shift) or by independent site-specific signals is not resolvable here. A:W is depleted at both BWM and control (BWM `log2_OR`=-0.70, control `log2_OR`=-0.74) but carries the `low-count` flag — the magnitude is statistically real but biologically unstable across replicates. Quote the direction (Trp at A site falls between d0 and d5 in both conditions), do not quote the magnitude. Worth carrying both into the codon sister CSV — A:K resolution onto AAG vs AAA, and A:W resolution onto TGG (Trp is single-codon, so no synonym-resolution available — the low-count flag is fundamental at this position).

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the E:K direction flip (BWM depleted vs control enriched, reproduces at d10_vs_d0 magnitude); the shared A:N enrichment at d5 (same direction in both arms, BWM ~2.5x larger; trajectories diverge by d10 — BWM sustained, control reverses); the BWM,E basic/polar redistribution (K/R depleted paired with Q/S enriched); control,E,P as a family-min `p_adj` cell with modest `log2_OR`=-0.63 — flag it as anti-conservative-amplified, do not quote p magnitude. *Exploratory*: the A:K near-flip (BWM near-sig depleted at `p_adj`=0.056 while control sig enriched); the BWM,P depletion landscape (K/G/R all clear FDR<0.05; check the codon sister for any synonym concentration). *Noise*: low-count flagged rows (A:W in both conditions; control,P,M) — direction reliable, magnitude not.

## Methods
Fisher's exact (two-sided) is applied to a 2x2 of (aa_count, total - aa_count) at each timepoint within one condition. The script (`stall_sites_non_consensus_stats.py`) computes BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of 20 tests for AA-level (so each of the 6 sub-families is corrected independently, not against the full 120). The test does **not** answer whether the same feature is changing in BWM and control in the same direction — that comparison is made by reading paired panels side-by-side (see "control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction" caveat) and is the largest-magnitude reading from this file's cells. Top hits are all rows with `p_adj` < 0.05 (family-level rule for `timepoint_fisher_within_condition`); no row cap and no raw-p fallback. Counts (`day_X_count`) and totals (`day_X_total`) are summed across replicates before the test, hence the family-level pseudoreplication caveat.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** 2x2 contingencies pool replicates by design; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative — read `log2_OR` as the primary effect column and treat the smallest p-magnitudes as inflated, not as evidence of additional certainty.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative.** control,E,P at the family-wide minimum `p_adj` on `log2_OR`=-0.63 is the most extreme single-feature large-N anti-conservative case in the family. Rank by `log2_OR` when ordering effects; do not over-read p magnitude.
- **bh-per-(condition,site).** BH is run independently within each of the 6 (condition, site) families of 20 AAs; two rows with the same raw p in different (condition, site) families can land at different `p_adj` — rank within a (condition, site) cell, not across the full 120-test grid.
- **within-condition-clean.** No condition pooling and no timepoint pooling; the file structurally answers "does day_5 differ from day_0 *within* this condition" cleanly, leaving the BWM-vs-control comparison to be done by reading paired panels side-by-side rather than by the test itself.
- **rare-aa-low-count.** Three Top-hits cells have small per-timepoint counts: BWM,A,W (108 vs 89), control,A,W (93 vs 382), control,P,M (186 vs 607). Direction is reliable; magnitude unstable for these rows.
- **OR-direction-asymmetry.** No zero-cell rows in this file. Caveat preserved as ranking-axis discipline only — `log2_OR` is the reported effect column (already log-transformed), so positive/negative bands are symmetric by construction; rank directly on it.
- **control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction.** Three large-magnitude direction flips flagged for downstream reconciliation:
  - **E:K** — BWM `log2_OR`=-0.42 vs control `log2_OR`=+0.26; direction flip. Reproduces the d10_vs_d0 finding (BWM `log2_OR`=-0.34 / control `log2_OR`=+0.18).
  - **A:K** — BWM `log2_OR`=-0.17 (`p_adj`=0.056, near-sig depleted) vs control `log2_OR`=+0.33; divergent direction, control side decisively significant.
  - **E:A** — BWM `log2_OR`=+0.02 ns vs control `log2_OR`=-0.36; control-only depletion.
  - Several control-only-significant cells where BWM trends the same direction but stays ns (E:P depleted, A:L depleted, A:I enriched, A:P depleted). control,P,V enriched is the one same-arm cell *not* matched at BWM — BWM,P,V `log2_OR`=-0.01 is essentially flat, so P:V is a control-only signal rather than a same-direction trend.
- **control-E-P-extreme-anticonservative.** control,E,P (Pro at site E, depleted) carries the family-wide minimum `p_adj` on a `log2_OR` of -0.63 (~35% relative depletion only). Cap rhetoric on this row: rank by `log2_OR` and interpret this row's p magnitude as direction-only; effect size carries the biological scale. Cross-check against `between_condition_wilcoxon_aa` (E:P p_adj=0.27 ns there — the time-collapsed MW does not see this signal because it is a within-control time response, not a perturbation effect).

---

## Plots — individual

![Day-5-vs-day-0 BWM A-site amino-acid volcano: log2(OR) on x-axis (range +/-1), -log10(FDR) on y. N (Asn, polar purple) at log2(OR) ~= +0.83, -log10(FDR) ~= 7.5 sits at the top-right as the largest enriched FDR<0.05 hit (the panel's tallest point). D (Asp, acidic red) at log2(OR) ~= +0.38, -log10(FDR) ~= 3.5 and F (Phe, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= +0.42, -log10(FDR) ~= 2.5 are the next-highest enriched. Y (Tyr, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= -0.46, -log10(FDR) ~= 3.3, G (Gly, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~= -0.32, -log10(FDR) ~= 2.5, and W (Trp, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= -0.70, -log10(FDR) ~= 2.5 are the FDR<0.05 depleted hits. 6 amino acids cross FDR<0.05 total.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/individual/BWM_A_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 BWM P-site amino-acid volcano: K (Lys, basic blue) at log2(OR) ~= -0.33, -log10(FDR) ~= 3.7 sits as the single high-y depleted outlier (the panel's tallest point). G (Gly, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~= -0.30, -log10(FDR) ~= 2.6 and R (Arg, basic blue) at log2(OR) ~= -0.30, -log10(FDR) ~= 1.8 are the next-highest FDR<0.05 depleted hits. Q (Gln, polar purple) at log2(OR) ~= +0.37, -log10(FDR) ~= 1.6 is the single FDR<0.05 enriched hit. 4 amino acids cross FDR<0.05 total — all on the depleted side except Q. Range tight (+/-0.5 log2(OR) for sig points).](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/individual/BWM_P_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 BWM E-site amino-acid volcano: K (Lys, basic blue) at log2(OR) ~= -0.42, -log10(FDR) ~= 8.7 sits as the file-minimum BWM cell and the largest depleted hit; this is the BWM half of the file's headline E:K direction flip. Q (Gln, polar purple) at log2(OR) ~= +0.59, -log10(FDR) ~= 5.1 and S (Ser, polar purple) at log2(OR) ~= +0.51, -log10(FDR) ~= 4.0 are the largest enriched FDR<0.05 hits. R (Arg, basic blue) at log2(OR) ~= -0.40, -log10(FDR) ~= 4.0 is the second depleted hit. G (Gly, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~= -0.26, -log10(FDR) ~= 1.8 sits just above the FDR threshold. 5 amino acids cross FDR<0.05 — basic/polar redistribution dominates the panel.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/individual/BWM_E_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 control A-site amino-acid volcano: the most populated panel in this CSV. K (Lys, basic blue) at log2(OR) ~= +0.33, -log10(FDR) ~= 7.2 sits at the top-right as the largest enriched hit; this is the control half of the A:K direction near-flip with BWM. L (Leu, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= -0.35, -log10(FDR) ~= 6.3 and Y (Tyr, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= -0.40, -log10(FDR) ~= 5.1 are the largest depleted hits. I (Ile, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= +0.32, -log10(FDR) ~= 5.1; N (Asn, polar purple) at log2(OR) ~= +0.33, -log10(FDR) ~= 3.9; W (Trp, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= -0.74, -log10(FDR) ~= 4.7; P (Pro, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~= -0.37, -log10(FDR) ~= 4.4; A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= -0.29, -log10(FDR) ~= 3.4; and E (Glu, acidic red) at log2(OR) ~= +0.14, -log10(FDR) ~= 1.7 round out the 9 FDR<0.05 hits.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/individual/control_A_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 control P-site amino-acid volcano: V (Val, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= +0.26, -log10(FDR) ~= 3.2 sits at the top as the largest enriched hit. P (Pro, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~= -0.30, -log10(FDR) ~= 2.6 and M (Met, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= -0.40, -log10(FDR) ~= 2.2 are the largest depleted hits. H (His, basic blue) at log2(OR) ~= -0.33, -log10(FDR) ~= 2.0; K (Lys, basic blue) at log2(OR) ~= -0.18, -log10(FDR) ~= 2.0; and E (Glu, acidic red) at log2(OR) ~= +0.15, -log10(FDR) ~= 1.7 round out the 6 FDR<0.05 hits. Range tight (+/-0.5 log2(OR)).](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/individual/control_P_volcano.png)

![Day-5-vs-day-0 control E-site amino-acid volcano: P (Pro, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~= -0.63, -log10(FDR) ~= 11.6 sits at the top-left as the file-minimum p_adj cell (the family-wide minimum across `timepoint_fisher_within_condition`); flagged as `control-E-P-extreme-anticonservative` — the magnitude is only ~35% relative depletion despite the extreme p. K (Lys, basic blue) at log2(OR) ~= +0.26, -log10(FDR) ~= 6.1 sits as the second-tallest enriched hit; this is the control half of the file's headline E:K direction flip with BWM. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= -0.36, -log10(FDR) ~= 4.2 and E (Glu, acidic red) at log2(OR) ~= +0.23, -log10(FDR) ~= 3.7 are the next-highest hits. T (Thr, polar purple) at log2(OR) ~= -0.23, -log10(FDR) ~= 1.9 and R (Arg, basic blue) at log2(OR) ~= +0.18, -log10(FDR) ~= 1.7 sit at the FDR threshold. 6 amino acids cross FDR<0.05.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d5_vs_d0/individual/control_E_volcano.png)
